description: Coordinate customer lifecycle outreach with LLM-generated messaging and delivery controls.
name: Customer Lifecycle Email Copilot

product.agent_role: Campaign planner, copywriter, policy checker, delivery executor, inbox responder.
product.customizable_for: Retention campaigns, onboarding journeys, renewal nudges, education sequences.
product.customize: Replace the sample customer snapshot with CRM data, add lifecycle events, tune policy gates, and connect approved email or Slack providers.
product.example: Synthetic customer snapshots flow through campaign planning, copy generation, policy checks, email execution, and reply handling.
product.investor: This can become a vertical lifecycle-growth product because it connects agent reasoning to real delivery systems and customer state.
product.one_line: Coordinate customer lifecycle outreach with LLM-generated messaging and delivery controls.
product.output: Prepared emails, sent-event logs, reply handling events, and campaign summaries.
product.problem: Customer outreach changes as replies, segments, policies, and delivery state evolve; static templates miss timing, context, and governance.
product.simulation_type: Lifecycle campaign loop with inbox and delivery state.
product.target_users: Growth, lifecycle marketing, and customer success teams.

description: Evaluate price changes against competitor moves, inventory pressure, and revenue impact.
name: Dynamic Pricing Profit Optimizer

product.customizable_for: Retail, travel, marketplaces, subscriptions, manufacturing quotes.
product.customize: Replace synthetic demand with commerce, competitor, inventory, and margin data; tune elasticity, guardrails, and promotion rules.
product.example: A connected thermostat SKU faces competitor moves and inventory pressure, and the agent chooses price increase, discount, or match actions.
product.investor: Dynamic pricing has direct profit impact and is a strong vertical for agentic simulation with human-reviewable recommendations.
product.one_line: Evaluate price changes against competitor moves, inventory pressure, and revenue impact.
product.output: Pricing recommendation with revenue and inventory tradeoffs.
product.problem: Pricing is dynamic and coupled: competitors, demand, inventory, and margin move together faster than static rules can explain.
product.simulation_type: Demand, competitor price, inventory, and revenue simulation.
product.target_users: Pricing teams, category managers, and revenue management leaders.

description: Monitor facility video streams and escalate safety-relevant observations with LLM reasoning.
name: Facility Safety Video Guardian

product.customizable_for: Door monitoring, restricted-area alerts, retail safety, warehouse operations.
product.customize: Replace the sample video, tune sampling cadence and alert cooldown, add site-specific safety policies, and connect security notification channels.
product.example: A door-camera stream is sampled, an Ollama-backed agent checks whether a person is visible, and alerts are throttled to avoid noise.
product.investor: This illustrates vertical AI for physical operations: agents reason over evolving sensor state and trigger controlled workflows.
product.one_line: Monitor facility video streams and escalate safety-relevant observations with LLM reasoning.
product.output: Detection events, alert decisions, and notification payloads.
product.problem: Video monitoring is continuous, noisy, and operationally sensitive; teams need stateful alerts, cooldowns, and explainable observations rather than raw frames.
product.simulation_type: Video stream sampling with alert state.
product.target_users: Facilities, security, safety operations, and property management teams.

description: Compare retention interventions before deploying them to at-risk customers.
name: Revenue Retention Copilot

product.customizable_for: SaaS retention, subscription commerce, financial services, telecom.
product.customize: Replace synthetic health metrics with CRM, product usage, support tickets, billing, and NPS data; tune action costs and segment rules.
product.example: A mid-market SaaS segment deteriorates, and the agent chooses success calls, loyalty offers, or product-fix campaigns.
product.investor: Retention is measurable and budgeted; this blueprint can evolve into a revenue-copilot product tied to CRM workflows.
product.one_line: Compare retention interventions before deploying them to at-risk customers.
product.output: Retention intervention plan with expected churn and budget tradeoffs.
product.problem: Churn risk changes with sentiment, product issues, and budget constraints; one-shot LLM suggestions do not show cost or state tradeoffs.
product.target_users: Revenue operations, customer success, and growth teams.

description: Plan staffing and deflection actions as service demand, queue waits, and SLA risk change.
name: Service Capacity Command Center

product.customizable_for: Call centers, field service, support queues, hospital operations.
product.customize: Connect ACD/WFM systems, interval forecasts, schedule data, abandonment curves, and skill-based routing constraints.
product.example: Call arrivals rise, wait time crosses the SLA line, and the agent chooses overtime, staff shifting, or self-service deflection.
product.investor: Service capacity is a concrete buyer problem with clear ROI in SLA, staffing cost, and customer satisfaction.
product.one_line: Plan staffing and deflection actions as service demand, queue waits, and SLA risk change.
product.output: Staffing forecast and capacity action plan.
product.problem: Staffing decisions are expensive and time-sensitive; dashboards show queue waits but do not simulate next-interval choices.
product.target_users: Contact-center leaders, workforce managers, and customer operations teams.

description: Test disruption responses before inventory, supplier delays, and customer service levels break down.
name: Supply Chain Resilience War Room

product.customizable_for: Manufacturing, retail, healthcare supplies, logistics networks.
product.customize: Connect ERP, supplier EDI, demand forecasts, inventory systems, and lane-level costs; tune service-level and delay assumptions.
product.example: Industrial sensor inventory tightens in the Northeast while delays rise, and the agent chooses expedite, supplier switch, or allocation actions.
product.investor: Supply-chain resilience is a large enterprise pain point where simulation plus LLM recommendations can become an operations command center.
product.one_line: Test disruption responses before inventory, supplier delays, and customer service levels break down.
product.output: Disruption response plan with action history and supplier-lane ranking.
product.problem: Demand spikes and supplier delays compound over time; static dashboards show the fire but do not test mitigation paths.
product.target_users: Supply chain operators, procurement leaders, and operations executives.

description: Prioritize insurance claim reviews as fraud signals, queue pressure, and adjuster capacity change.
name: Claim Risk Triage Copilot

product.customizable_for: Auto, property, health, warranty, and specialty claims.
product.customize: Replace synthetic claims with FNOL, adjuster notes, photos, repair estimates, policy data, and fraud-model outputs.
product.example: Auto claims volume rises, fraud signal spikes, and the agent chooses SIU escalation, fast-track, or evidence requests.
product.investor: Claims triage has measurable ROI in leakage, cycle time, and customer satisfaction, making it a strong vertical agent workflow.
product.one_line: Prioritize insurance claim reviews as fraud signals, queue pressure, and adjuster capacity change.
product.output: Claim triage report with prioritized segments and review actions.
product.problem: Claims queues evolve continuously; teams need to balance fast settlement, fraud risk, loss ratio, and adjuster capacity.
product.target_users: Insurance claims leaders, SIU teams, adjuster operations, and insurtech builders.

description: Detect rising borrower default pressure and compare credit policy responses over time.
name: Credit Default Early Warning System

product.customizable_for: Consumer lending, SMB credit, cards, mortgage prequalification.
product.customize: Replace synthetic metrics with bureau attributes, payment history, application data, macro variables, and institution-specific approval policies.
product.example: Default rates rise in a consumer installment portfolio, and the agent weighs tightening credit against risk-based pricing.
product.investor: Credit risk is a regulated, high-ROI vertical where stateful AI can support explainable early-warning workflows.
product.one_line: Detect rising borrower default pressure and compare credit policy responses over time.
product.output: Credit risk decision report with policy action history.
product.problem: Borrower behavior, rates, approvals, and yield interact over time; static scorecards miss policy tradeoffs under changing conditions.
product.simulation_type: Borrower default and credit policy simulation.
product.target_users: Credit-risk teams, lenders, fintech underwriters, and portfolio analysts.

description: Watch simulated market microstructure and produce live liquidity-risk explanations.
name: Liquidity Microstructure Radar

product.agent_role: Signal analyzer, market explainer, advisor agent.
product.customizable_for: Equities, crypto, FX, liquidity monitoring, trading operations.
product.customize: Replace the fake stock stream with exchange, broker, or market-data feeds; tune symbols, volatility, thresholds, and alert policy.
product.example: A synthetic equity stream generates ticks, technical signals, market-advisor events, and LLM explanations for meaningful moves.
product.investor: The blueprint is a credible wedge for trading-risk copilots because it combines streaming data, simulation, and explainable agent decisions.
product.one_line: Watch simulated market microstructure and produce live liquidity-risk explanations.
product.output: Market signals, risk explanations, advice events, and optional Slack alerts.
product.problem: Markets evolve tick by tick; spreads, depth, momentum, and volatility interact faster than static dashboards or one-shot summaries can capture.
product.simulation_type: Live market stream and exchange-style signal simulation.
product.target_users: Trading teams, market-risk analysts, and fintech product evaluators.

description: Stress a portfolio against drawdown, rate shocks, and liquidity pressure before recommending rebalancing.
name: Portfolio Crash Stress Lab

product.customizable_for: Wealth management, treasury, family offices, robo-advisory risk overlays.
product.customize: Connect holdings, factor exposures, risk models, macro scenarios, tax constraints, and policy limits.
product.example: A stagflation shock raises rates and drawdown while liquidity falls, and the agent chooses hedge, cash, or defensive rebalance actions.
product.investor: Asset-management risk copilots are commercially attractive because decisions are high-value and require explainable scenario reasoning.
product.one_line: Stress a portfolio against drawdown, rate shocks, and liquidity pressure before recommending rebalancing.
product.output: Portfolio risk report and rebalance recommendation.
product.problem: Risk changes path-dependently during shocks; one summary cannot show how hedges, cash, and defensive moves alter the next state.
product.simulation_type: Macro shock and portfolio risk simulation.
product.target_users: Portfolio managers, wealth advisors, risk officers, and fintech evaluators.

description: Rank property acquisition opportunities as ZIP-code demand, cap rate, price, and risk evolve.
name: Zip Code Property Alpha Engine

product.customizable_for: Rental acquisitions, flipping pipelines, multifamily sourcing, market expansion.
product.customize: Replace synthetic listings with MLS, county records, broker flow, Redfin/Zillow exports, rent data, and investor constraints like DSCR or cap-rate floors.
product.example: A target ZIP code sees demand tighten and risk widen, and the agent decides whether to bid, negotiate, or watchlist listings.
product.investor: This is a credible vertical AI wedge for real-estate investors because the output is a ranked, actionable acquisition list.
product.one_line: Rank property acquisition opportunities as ZIP-code demand, cap rate, price, and risk evolve.
product.output: Ranked opportunities and bid/watchlist recommendation.
product.problem: Property opportunities change with rates, inventory, rents, neighborhood demand, and liquidity; static comps cannot rank next-best actions.
product.target_users: Real-estate investors, acquisition analysts, and property-tech teams.

description: Show the core runtime pattern: an LLM agent observes a changing queue and acts over repeated ticks.
name: Closed Loop Agent Runtime

product.customizable_for: Support queues, claim processing, review pipelines, fulfillment operations.
product.customize: Replace queue metrics with your workflow KPIs, add actions, change the scoring heuristic, and connect the final artifact to a dashboard.
product.example: A work queue grows, capacity and quality drift, and an operations agent chooses whether to add capacity, protect quality, or hold policy.
product.investor: This is the smallest proof of the platform thesis: agents reason inside systems, not outside them.
product.one_line: Show the core runtime pattern: an LLM agent observes a changing queue and acts over repeated ticks.
product.output: Simulation summary with action history, state deltas, and next steps.
product.problem: Most LLM demos stop after one answer; real operations require repeated observations, decisions, state updates, and traceable outcomes.
product.target_users: Developers, evaluators, and technical buyers learning the closed-loop agent model.

description: Run role-scoped context packets through a multi-agent audit decision workflow.
name: Context Memory Audit Pipeline

product.agent_role: Policy interpreter, evidence extractor, risk classifier, decision agent, critic.
product.customizable_for: Compliance review, legal intake, regulated operations, internal controls.
product.customize: Replace the synthetic audit transcript, policy text, ACL roles, memory item schemas, and final decision rubric.
product.example: Policy, evidence, risk, decision, and critic agents each receive only the context packet they are allowed to see.
product.investor: Governed memory is a core enterprise requirement for regulated AI workflows; this blueprint makes it concrete.
product.one_line: Run role-scoped context packets through a multi-agent audit decision workflow.
product.output: Traceable audit decision and critic report.
product.problem: Agents should not all see the same raw context; serious workflows need scoped memory, traceability, and final decisions that cite structured evidence.
product.simulation_type: Multi-agent context handoff with scoped memory.
product.target_users: Developers and compliance teams evaluating context-engine governance.

description: Stress-test compressed working memory across a growing multi-agent LLM workflow.
name: Context Memory Compression Lab

product.agent_role: Context researcher, expander, challenger, compressor, briefing author.
product.customizable_for: Research copilots, investigation workflows, case management, long-running planning agents.
product.customize: Change token budgets, pinned terms, memory item types, compression policy, and model-compression settings.
product.example: Agents create larger artifacts over several turns while CompileContext keeps each packet bounded and traceable.
product.investor: State management and compression are differentiators for agent platforms moving beyond one-shot prompts.
product.one_line: Stress-test compressed working memory across a growing multi-agent LLM workflow.
product.output: Compressed context handoff and final briefing.
product.problem: Long-running agents accumulate more context than a model can safely consume; compression must preserve pinned facts, constraints, and decision traces.
product.simulation_type: Growing memory graph with compression pressure.
product.target_users: Developers building long-context agent systems with bounded prompt budgets.

description: Control a changing environment where load, temperature, reserve, and service levels interact.
name: Dynamic Environment Control Loop

product.customizable_for: Energy systems, cloud capacity, industrial operations, logistics control rooms.
product.customize: Replace metrics with energy, infrastructure, compute, or logistics state; add constraints and domain-specific control actions.
product.example: Load rises, reserves fall, and the controller chooses whether to charge, shed load, or hold steady.
product.investor: This demonstrates why runtime state and simulation matter for agentic control applications.
product.one_line: Control a changing environment where load, temperature, reserve, and service levels interact.
product.output: Control summary with metric trajectory and recommended action.
product.problem: Operational systems are coupled and time-varying; one-shot advice cannot show how a control action changes the next state.
product.target_users: Developers and operators testing control-loop blueprints before adding domain data.

description: Triage event streams as anomaly pressure, queue depth, and false positives change over time.
name: Event Stream Triage State Machine

product.customizable_for: Security operations, payments monitoring, reliability engineering, fraud detection.
product.customize: Replace synthetic events with logs, payments, telemetry, or security alerts; tune anomaly thresholds and routing actions.
product.example: An event stream spikes, anomaly scores rise, and the triage agent tightens filters or escalates high-signal events.
product.investor: This maps directly to commercial observability and security use cases where stateful triage is valuable.
product.one_line: Triage event streams as anomaly pressure, queue depth, and false positives change over time.
product.output: Event stream report with queue state, triage actions, and ranked partitions.
product.problem: Event streams are noisy and bursty; teams need agents that maintain state and route actions as conditions change.
product.target_users: Developers building alerting, stream processing, and incident triage agents.

description: Insert human approval into an LLM decision loop before high-impact actions are applied.
name: Human Approval Decision Gate

product.customizable_for: Risk approvals, spend controls, compliance decisions, operational escalations.
product.customize: Replace the approval policy, add approver roles, connect ticketing systems, and define which actions require human sign-off.
product.example: Risk exposure rises, the agent recommends mitigation, and a simulated approval gate determines whether the action applies.
product.investor: Human-in-the-loop control is essential for enterprise adoption, especially in regulated or high-cost workflows.
product.one_line: Insert human approval into an LLM decision loop before high-impact actions are applied.
product.output: Approval memo with recommended and applied actions.
product.problem: Enterprise agents often recommend actions that should not execute automatically; approval state must be part of the runtime loop.
product.target_users: Developers and governance teams designing approval-gated agent workflows.

description: Move live telemetry chunks through stream workers and summarize the evolving signal.
name: Live Telemetry Stream Pipeline

product.customizable_for: IoT telemetry, application logs, market data, operational sensors.
product.customize: Replace the telemetry generator, add stream partitions, tune event cadence, and extend the summarizer for your metrics.
product.example: Synthetic telemetry events are generated, processed by a stream worker, and summarized into signal peaks and state changes.
product.investor: Stream processing is where static prompts fail; this blueprint shows agents embedded in live data movement.
product.one_line: Move live telemetry chunks through stream workers and summarize the evolving signal.
product.output: Signal summary and stream result artifacts.
product.problem: Streaming systems require backpressure-aware routing, stateful workers, and outputs that remain interpretable as events arrive.
product.target_users: Developers building event-stream workflows and runtime observability paths.

description: Show an LLM agent calling a forecast tool before deciding how to rebalance resources.
name: Llm Tool Orchestration Loop

product.customizable_for: Demand planning, capacity management, operational forecasting, scheduling.
product.customize: Replace the moving-window forecast with your model, add tools, change action schemas, and log tool traces for evaluation.
product.example: A demand forecast tool predicts pressure, and the agent chooses whether to rebalance or add buffer.
product.investor: Tool orchestration is a bridge from chatbot to workflow engine; this blueprint makes that transition inspectable.
product.one_line: Show an LLM agent calling a forecast tool before deciding how to rebalance resources.
product.output: Tool-use trace and resource recommendation.
product.problem: LLMs are more useful when they can combine reasoning with tools and then observe whether the tool-informed decision worked.
product.target_users: Developers adding tools, forecasts, or deterministic calculators to agent workflows.

description: Trace how messages move through router and aggregator agents.
name: Message Routing Trace

product.customizable_for: Graph prototyping, training, runtime smoke checks.
product.customize: Add message types, branch routes, filters, or reducers to model your graph topology before adding domain logic.
product.example: A synthetic request moves from ingress to router to reducer so users can inspect routing semantics end to end.
product.investor: Clear routing primitives make the platform easier to adopt, debug, and govern.
product.one_line: Trace how messages move through router and aggregator agents.
product.output: Completed route with aggregated payload.
product.problem: Agent graphs are hard to debug unless developers can see message types, edges, and completion behavior clearly.
product.target_users: Developers learning the runtime message model.

description: Model buyer-supplier negotiation as agents respond to changing demand, price, and capacity.
name: Multi Agent Contract Negotiation Loop

product.customizable_for: Procurement, renewals, vendor management, enterprise sales deal desks.
product.customize: Replace synthetic terms with contracts, supplier scorecards, price corridors, delivery constraints, or approval rules.
product.example: Buyer and supplier messages are generated each tick while a mediator chooses offer, volume, or pause actions.
product.investor: The blueprint shows how the runtime can represent multiple stakeholder agents, a core pattern for enterprise decision automation.
product.one_line: Model buyer-supplier negotiation as agents respond to changing demand, price, and capacity.
product.output: Negotiation brief with agreement score, action history, and ranked deal terms.
product.problem: Negotiation is dynamic: positions change as demand, capacity, and costs move, so static summaries cannot capture the next best move.
product.target_users: Developers building multi-agent workflows and procurement teams evaluating negotiation automation.

description: Observe a fast native-agent live monitor that keeps producing decisions until stopped.
name: Native Live Monitor Daemon

product.customizable_for: Operations telemetry, rules engines, high-frequency synthetic simulations.
product.customize: Replace the divisibility rule with your own fast rule engine, alert threshold, or lightweight simulation process.
product.example: A divisibility monitor streams generated values, evaluates conditions, and reports live state through native runtime agents.
product.investor: The runtime is not limited to slow external calls; native agents can run high-frequency loops alongside LLM workflows.
product.one_line: Observe a fast native-agent live monitor that keeps producing decisions until stopped.
product.output: Streaming monitor events and summaries.
product.problem: Some simulations and monitors need low-overhead native agents that can run continuously and emit structured updates.
product.target_users: Runtime evaluators comparing native BEAM agents with external worker processes.

description: Run isolated shell and Python workers as a traceable runtime pipeline.
name: Openshell Sandbox Worker Pipeline

product.customizable_for: ETL tasks, compliance checks, code validation, document processing.
product.customize: Replace the word-count scripts with domain workers, add policies for allowed egress, tune pool slots, and extend the output schema for your artifacts.
product.example: A developer sends a text payload to a shell metrics worker, routes the result into a Python report worker, and receives a structured completion event.
product.investor: This shows that the runtime can orchestrate ordinary enterprise tools safely, which is essential for deploying agent workflows inside controlled environments.
product.one_line: Run isolated shell and Python workers as a traceable runtime pipeline.
product.output: Worker metrics and a Python-generated report.
product.problem: Teams need to run untrusted or heterogeneous tasks without losing observability, output contracts, or retry behavior.
product.simulation_type: Static worker pipeline with deterministic generated input.
product.target_users: Developers evaluating sandboxed worker execution and artifact handoff patterns.

description: Stress the runtime with a broad fan-out of deterministic parallel workers.
name: Parallel Worker Scale Benchmark

product.customizable_for: Batch analytics, Monte Carlo jobs, large enrichment queues.
product.customize: Change worker count, payload size, task duration, or reducer logic to match your capacity benchmark.
product.example: The runtime fans out numeric range checks, collects worker outputs, and summarizes completion across many executor jobs.
product.investor: Scale and reliability are commercial proof points; this blueprint makes orchestration capacity visible.
product.one_line: Stress the runtime with a broad fan-out of deterministic parallel workers.
product.output: Scale summary and worker completion metrics.
product.problem: Agent systems need to prove they can dispatch many bounded tasks and collect results reliably before being trusted with larger workloads.
product.target_users: Platform teams testing scheduler throughput, worker pools, and aggregation behavior.

description: Tune policy thresholds through repeated simulated reward and incident feedback.
name: Policy Feedback Optimization Loop

product.customizable_for: Fraud thresholds, safety policies, routing rules, pricing guardrails.
product.customize: Replace reward functions, add constraints, change policy actions, and compare multiple policy candidates.
product.example: A policy threshold changes as reward, incident rate, and coverage move across simulated episodes.
product.investor: Policy optimization is a high-leverage runtime pattern for safety, fraud, routing, and operations products.
product.one_line: Tune policy thresholds through repeated simulated reward and incident feedback.
product.output: Policy recommendation with reward trajectory and action history.
product.problem: Policies that look good once can fail over time; users need to see reward, coverage, and incidents evolve under each adjustment.
product.target_users: Developers and policy owners testing adaptive decision rules.

description: Run a Python-defined workflow as a long-lived daemon with repeated stateful turns.
name: Python Sdk Live Research Daemon

product.customizable_for: Incident monitors, market watchers, research crawlers.
product.customize: Adjust daemon cadence, add data sources, persist state, and specialize the Python agents for your monitor or research loop.
product.example: A daemon periodically collects signals, decides whether more work is needed, and emits runtime events until cancelled.
product.investor: Long-lived loops are a key difference between agent runtimes and chat interfaces; this blueprint shows that operating model directly.
product.one_line: Run a Python-defined workflow as a long-lived daemon with repeated stateful turns.
product.output: Ongoing events and final cancellation-safe state.
product.problem: Many agent workflows need to keep running, react to new events, and maintain operational state rather than finish after one request.
product.simulation_type: Live daemon loop with repeated inputs.
product.target_users: Developers building live monitors, scheduled analysis loops, or long-running research agents.

description: Define a MirrorNeuron workflow directly in Python and compile it into a runnable bundle.
name: Python Sdk Research Pipeline

product.customizable_for: Internal research, report generation, enrichment workflows.
product.customize: Edit `workflow.py`, swap in your own data retrieval function, add more agents, and regenerate the bundle with `python -m mn_blueprint_support.python_workflow_bundle_cli`.
product.example: A research topic flows through ingress, retrieval, and review agents before a final summary artifact is saved.
product.investor: The SDK path lowers adoption friction: teams can start from normal Python and graduate to runtime-managed agent graphs.
product.one_line: Define a MirrorNeuron workflow directly in Python and compile it into a runnable bundle.
product.output: Saved research summary and final workflow result.
product.problem: Developers need a low-friction path from Python functions to distributed runtime agents while keeping retry, timeout, and graph semantics explicit.
product.target_users: Python developers who want agent workflows without hand-writing manifests.

description: Generate, review, and validate code through a sandboxed multi-step LLM loop.
name: Sandboxed Llm Codegen Review Loop

product.customizable_for: Internal developer tools, data scripts, code repair, controlled automation.
product.customize: Change the coding task, validator tests, review rubric, sandbox policy, model, or allowed toolchain.
product.example: The LLM writes a Python tool, another LLM review step critiques it, and a validator executes tests inside an OpenShell sandbox.
product.investor: This is a credible path to enterprise coding agents because it combines reasoning, iteration, and sandboxed execution controls.
product.one_line: Generate, review, and validate code through a sandboxed multi-step LLM loop.
product.output: Generated code, review history, and validation result.
product.problem: One-shot code generation is risky; production teams need review, validation, isolation, and iterative improvement before executing generated code.
product.target_users: Engineering teams evaluating safe LLM coding workflows.

description: Track simulation state deltas so every agent decision is explainable after the run.
name: Simulation State Audit Trail

product.customizable_for: Regulated operations, simulations, workflow audits, model evaluations.
product.customize: Add domain state fields, checkpoints, persistence, audit metadata, and evaluation metrics for your environment.
product.example: State drift increases, confidence changes, and the agent chooses checkpoints or stabilization actions while preserving a timeline.
product.investor: Traceability turns agent simulations into enterprise-grade decision records rather than black-box demos.
product.one_line: Track simulation state deltas so every agent decision is explainable after the run.
product.output: State tracking report with deltas and ranked subsystems.
product.problem: Dynamic systems are hard to trust unless users can see what changed, why an action was chosen, and how the next state moved.
product.target_users: Developers and evaluators who need inspectable state transitions.

description: Show bounded queues, slow workers, and retry-later behavior in a live stream.
name: Stream Backpressure Control Loop

product.agent_role: Live source, slow processor, metrics sink.
product.customizable_for: Customer events, telemetry, alerts, market ticks.
product.customize: Tune queue sizes, worker latency, retry thresholds, and sink metrics to match your stream reliability goals.
product.example: A live source produces events faster than a slow worker can process them, and the runtime emits backpressure and retry signals.
product.investor: Backpressure is production plumbing, not a demo flourish; this blueprint proves the runtime is built for real systems.
product.one_line: Show bounded queues, slow workers, and retry-later behavior in a live stream.
product.output: Backpressure metrics and stream processing report.
product.problem: Real event streams overload workers; agent runtimes need visible backpressure and safe rejection semantics.
product.target_users: Platform teams designing reliable live agent systems.

description: Select the next lab experiment from prior simulated yield, toxicity, cost, and evidence confidence.
name: Adaptive Experiment Discovery Agent

product.customizable_for: Biology, chemistry, materials, formulation, process optimization.
product.customize: Replace synthetic outcomes with LIMS/ELN data, assay results, cost models, safety constraints, and Bayesian or active-learning tools.
product.example: A synthetic enzyme candidate shows mixed yield and toxicity, and the scientist agent chooses pH refinement, temperature sweep, or catalyst switch.
product.investor: Adaptive experimentation is a high-value scientific workflow where closed-loop agents can shorten discovery cycles.
product.one_line: Select the next lab experiment from prior simulated yield, toxicity, cost, and evidence confidence.
product.output: Experiment optimization plan with ranked experiment options.
product.problem: Experiment planning is sequential; each result changes the next best experiment and the cost-risk tradeoff.
product.target_users: Lab researchers, R&D teams, computational scientists, and scientific AI builders.

description: Plan local flood mitigations as rainfall, flood level, pump capacity, and vulnerable assets change.
name: Climate Resilience Planning Engine

product.customizable_for: Cities, utilities, campuses, hospitals, industrial sites.
product.customize: Replace synthetic weather with forecasts, flood maps, asset inventories, pump telemetry, road networks, and local cost assumptions.
product.example: Rainfall intensifies in Riverton, flood levels rise, and the advisor chooses pumps, sandbags, or road closures.
product.investor: Climate adaptation budgets are growing, and this blueprint shows a credible planning engine for local infrastructure decisions.
product.one_line: Plan local flood mitigations as rainfall, flood level, pump capacity, and vulnerable assets change.
product.output: Local climate mitigation plan with ranked assets.
product.problem: Climate events unfold over time and stress infrastructure unevenly; static risk maps do not recommend operational mitigations during a scenario.
product.simulation_type: Weather, flooding, and infrastructure vulnerability simulation.
product.target_users: Municipal resilience teams, infrastructure planners, utilities, and climate-risk analysts.

description: Run a long-lived multi-stage drug-discovery loop that keeps generating and evaluating candidates.
name: Drug Discovery Closed Loop Lab

product.agent_role: Scientific manager and staged worker agents.
product.customizable_for: Drug discovery, material search, assay planning, scientific literature loops.
product.customize: Swap target data, scoring functions, candidate generators, stage policies, and stopping criteria.
product.example: A pipeline based on BioTarget runs staged workers that manage candidate discovery and result extraction in a continuing loop.
product.investor: Scientific discovery is a high-value vertical where closed-loop agent workflows can reduce cycle time if connected to validated models and labs.
product.one_line: Run a long-lived multi-stage drug-discovery loop that keeps generating and evaluating candidates.
product.output: Candidate artifacts, stage logs, and discovery summaries.
product.problem: Discovery workflows are iterative: candidate generation, scoring, extraction, and review need to repeat as new evidence arrives.
product.target_users: Computational biology researchers and scientific AI platform evaluators.

description: Explore ecosystem interventions across multiple regions with evolving population dynamics.
name: Ecosystem Intervention Sandbox

product.agent_role: World coordinator, regional agents, leaderboard summarizer.
product.customizable_for: Conservation planning, invasive species modeling, education, agent-based simulation.
product.customize: Change species traits, resource regeneration, migration rate, region topology, intervention policies, or final scoring.
product.example: A world agent distributes animals across regions, region agents evolve local populations, and a leaderboard summarizes outcomes.
product.investor: This demonstrates the runtime's ability to coordinate many local simulators into a global decision view, a pattern relevant beyond ecology.
product.one_line: Explore ecosystem interventions across multiple regions with evolving population dynamics.
product.output: Regional outcomes, population rankings, and ecosystem summary.
product.problem: Ecological systems change through local interactions, migration, resources, and mutation; interventions need scenario testing before field action.
product.target_users: Ecologists, conservation analysts, simulation researchers, and education teams.

description: Visualize shared-world multi-agent motion planning and coordination behavior.
name: Multi Agent Motion Planning Lab

product.customizable_for: Robotics, warehouse coordination, autonomous planning, teaching labs.
product.customize: Change environment geometry, agent policies, reward structure, visualization output, and planning constraints.
product.example: A shared-world particle environment is generated and summarized to show how agents coordinate around a target task.
product.investor: Multi-agent physical simulation is a foundation for robotics and autonomy workflows where stateful agents must coordinate over time.
product.one_line: Visualize shared-world multi-agent motion planning and coordination behavior.
product.output: Simulation summary and visual artifacts.
product.problem: Multi-agent coordination depends on changing positions, goals, and collisions; users need visual traces, not only scalar metrics.
product.target_users: Robotics researchers, simulation developers, and multi-agent systems educators.

description: Compare outbreak interventions as infections, mobility, vaccination, and hospital load change.
name: Outbreak Response Policy Simulator

product.customizable_for: Campus health, local agencies, hospital systems, workplace safety.
product.customize: Replace synthetic disease state with surveillance, mobility, vaccination, hospital, or wastewater data; tune transmission and intervention assumptions.
product.example: Infections rise in a 50,000-person population, and the advisor chooses vaccination, mobility reduction, or testing interventions.
product.investor: Public-sector and healthcare planning need explainable scenario tools; this shows an AI advisor inside a dynamic disease model.
product.one_line: Compare outbreak interventions as infections, mobility, vaccination, and hospital load change.
product.output: Public-health intervention plan with state trajectory.
product.problem: Outbreak response depends on time-varying spread and healthcare load; static dashboards cannot test policy consequences before action.
product.target_users: Public-health planners, epidemiology teams, campus operations, and policy analysts.

description: Test signal and rerouting controls as traffic speed, incidents, volume, and emissions evolve.
name: Urban Traffic Control Lab

product.customizable_for: Signal optimization, incident response, transit priority, event planning.
product.customize: Connect loop detectors, cameras, signal controllers, incident feeds, GTFS transit data, and emissions models.
product.example: Vehicle count rises and an incident worsens, so the agent selects green corridors, rerouting, or transit priority.
product.investor: Urban mobility is a visible vertical where simulation plus AI control recommendations can become a city operations product.
product.one_line: Test signal and rerouting controls as traffic speed, incidents, volume, and emissions evolve.
product.output: Traffic control plan with corridor ranking and action history.
product.problem: Traffic control decisions are coupled and time-sensitive; dashboards show congestion but do not reason over next-state control options.
product.target_users: City traffic teams, transportation researchers, smart-city operators, and mobility startups.
